verse.es: "Cada uno es tentado por su propia concupiscencia... y el pecado, una vez consumado, engendra muerte."
verse.en: "Each person is tempted when they are dragged away by their own desire... and sin, when fully grown, gives birth to death."

es: Dia 2. Vigila tu corazón no como policía, sino como centinela de lo que amas.
Trae esto al Santisimo sin mediaciones. Sin excusas. Sin palabras. Solo presencia.
La Escritura de hoy (Stg 1,14-15) no es informacion: es medicina. Tómala.
La tentacion es real. Tu libertad para rechazarla también.
en: Today, God knocks again at the door of your interior life with a concrete call: 'Recognize temptation'.
In silent prayer, receive this word for you: 'Temptation loses strength when you name it in My presence.'
Today's Scripture (Stg 1,14-15) gives a precise direction. Do not leave it as theory: translate it into one small, real, and measurable decision before the day ends.
If temptation or memory of sin appears, do not hide. Name it, reject the lie, and choose a concrete path of grace: prayer, accompaniment, sacrament, reparation.
Beginning: cut off one occasion of falling today. Conversion begins when you stop negotiating with what wounds you.

prayer.es: Senor Jesus, reconozco que sin Ti no puedo vencer la tentacion ni sanar mi pecado. Dame corazon humilde, fortaleza en la lucha, arrepentimiento sincero y perseverancia para caminar en tu gracia. Lavame, levantame y guame por senderos de santidad. Amen.
prayer.en: Lord Jesus, I acknowledge that without You I cannot overcome temptation or heal from sin. Give me a humble heart, strength in the struggle, sincere repentance, and perseverance to walk in Your grace. Wash me, raise me, and guide me on paths of holiness. Amen.

act.es: Hoy realiza un acto concreto de conversion: evita una ocasion de pecado, corta un contenido que te hace caer, busca reconciliacion, o agenda tu confesion sacramental.
act.en: Today do one concrete act of conversion: avoid an occasion of sin, cut off content that leads you to fall, seek reconciliation, or schedule your sacramental confession.
